programmes.show_description: 1977. Thriller. Directed by Robert Aldrich. Stars Burt Lancaster, Richard Widmark & Roscoe Lee Browne. An imprisoned rogue Air Force general seizes a nuclear silo and threatens to start WWIII.
1954. Romance. Directed by Paul Dickson. Stars Griffth Jones, Kathleen Byron & Hugh Williams. A cynical sculptor becomes inspired by a ballerina he meets, but this only enrages her jealous lover.
Romney Marsh/Pumpkins. 1986. Jack looks back at a sunny visit to Kent when he tried to photograph a 'flying frog' on Romney Marsh, and he visits a village pumpkin fair.
1946. Thriller. Director: Gordon Douglas. Stars Morgan Conway, Dick Wessel & Esther Howard. Dick Tracy is on the trail of the rampaging arch nemesis, Cueball.
The Stake. 1974. Stars Roberta Tovey, Brian Coburn & Simon Cuff. Lady Ann is put on the stand to determine whether she is guilty of witchcraft, but will Black Arrow return and save the day?
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. When the two strange boys arrive in his realm, King Nerada worries about the fulfilment of ancient prophecies.
The Magic Book. 1956. Stars William Russell, Ronald Leigh-Hunt & Cyril Smith. Sir Lancelot helps to keep safe an Abbey and the occupants from invaders.
The Duo Defy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze gives Professor Isaacson an ultimatum: meet his demands, or he will begin to freeze the entire country.
Outcasts of Paradise Valley. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger & Dale Evans. Unable to find a job, two young men decide on a life of crime. Their first victims are an elderly couple.
Big Ben Strikes Again. 1967. Stars Sylvia Anderson, Donald Gray & Janna Hill. The Mysterons announce they intend to destroy London and a truck carrying an atomic device goes missing in the city.
The Young Widow. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Having escaped from Gessler's troops, Tell and his wife are given refuge by the beautiful Countess von Marheim.
The Hand of the Hawk. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Anthony Dawson & Sid James. Tempest tries to solve the murder of a crew member from another ship. A mysterious clue may just hold the answer.
The Traitor. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. King John fails to gather money to release Richard, some Lords raise the money. Robin must secretly get it out the country.
1963. Family. Directed by Colin Bell. Stars Christopher Brett, Shirley Joy & Peter Butterworth. A group of children attempt to retrieve their toy plane from the top of an abandoned tower.
24 Hours. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Robert Arden & Joy Owen. In Piccadilly, Maguire spots Caldwell, a criminal he once arrested in Canada; he has a hunch that he is up to no good and follows him.

programmes.show_name: The Ship That Died of Shame
programmes.show_description: (AKA PT Raiders) 1955. Thriller. Director Basil Dearden. Stars Richard Attenborough, George Baker and Bill Owen. A trio of ex-servicemen begin smuggling black market items into post-war Britain.
1945. A short documentary about football. This film explores everything football such as teaching school children football techniques, scouting the next professional players and nationwide matches!
Dutchman's Reef. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Leonard Teale. Mrs. Culbert summons Cobb to search for her missing son but he is not interested until they come to an agreement.
1956. Drama. Directed by Charles Frend. Stars Jack Hawkins, John Stratton, Dorothy Alison & Michael Brooke. The sleuths of Scotland Yard try to solve a series of burglaries.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life High, Wide and Faster
programmes.show_description: 1963. A captivating look at the plans for closer co-ordination between our roads, railways and coastal shipping.
The Trap. 1962. Stars Rupert Davies, Sonia Dresdel & Jacqueline Hill. Maigret creates a trap to capture a serial killer who is targeting women in the streets of Montmartre.
1974. Thriller. Directed by Ronald Neame. Stars Jon Voight, Maximillian Schell & Maria Schell. A reporter tries to track down a number of SS officers who mysteriously vanished after the war.
1961. Crime. Director: Ernest Morris. Stars John Hewer, Ann Lynn & Jean Muir. A dancer collapses and dies on stage at the famous Flamingo Club leading her husband to delve into the investigation.
1994. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. Meet Switzerland's 78 year old rave star. Visit the German men's group who romp naked in the woods, inspired by Robert Bly's 'Iron John'.
1962. Comedy. Director: John Wallis. Stars Randy Brent, Gabrielle Benett & Marianne Bennett. A man lost in the desert comes across an oasis with six naked women.
1960. Drama. Directors: Larry Wolk & Doris Wishman. Stars Greg Conrad, Dolores Carlos & Earl Bauer. After robbing a bank, two brothers take a nudist woman hostage and hide out in a nudist camp.
Unseen for years this strange, B-Picture oddity sees Warren Mitchell superb as fairground magician Emilio Dinelli, tormented by a gang of rowdies who take him prisoner in his backstreet workshop.
Shepherd's War. 1956. Stars: William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Hearing that friendly shepherds are under attack by plundering knights, Sir Lancelot volunteers to help.
In Search of the Tajmanon. 1964. Stars Lois Maxwell, Ray Barrett & Don Mason. The crew aid Prof. Graham in his search for the temple of Tajmanon which was submerged when a dam was built in Africa.
1965. The Abominable Snowman. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. After agreeing to deliver a salesman's sample case, Honey becomes marked for death.
1962. Drama directed by Francis Searle and starring Dermot Walsh. When a young girl is knocked down there are only three donors of the same blood group. A frantic search to find the donors begins.

programmes.show_name: Voyage to the Planet of Prehistoric Women
programmes.show_description: 1968. Sci-fi. Director: Peter Bogdanovich. Stars Mamie Van Doren & Mary Marr. When a group of astronauts land on Venus they are surprised to find dangerous creatures and beautiful women.
1967. Western. Director: Sergio Corbucci. Stars Joseph Cotten, Norma Bengell & Julian Mateos. The Civil War is over, and the South has fallen, but Col. Jonas has a plan to revive the Confederacy.
Lafe Returns. 1964. Stars Buddy Ebsen, Peter Whitney & Bobs Watson. Still trying to get his hands on the Clampett millions, Lafe pays a return visit to try to find where Jed has buried his money.
1959. Sci-fi. Directed by Spencer Gordon Bennet. Stars Arthur Franz & Dick Foran. A submarine is sent out to investigate a series of naval disasters, but the crew also get caught up in the chaos.
Black Widow Strikes Again. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. The Black Widow uses a brain control device to rob several banks, can batman stop her? Or will she control him too?
1967. Western. Director: James Neilson. Stars Roddy McDowall, Suzanne Pleshette & Karl Malden. To restore his family's wealth, a young lad stows away on a ship bound for the California Gold Rush.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Bookies at the Crossroads
programmes.show_description: 1964. An interesting study of the present state of bookmakers in England, their possible future, and competition represented by the tote.
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Michael Barrymore, Melvyn Hayes, Barry Howard, Ruth Madoc, Leslie Ash & Adrienne Posta.
The Dove Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo steals a dove-shaped medal from the leader of a European nation. The medal has information about Thrush.
The Spinners of The Spools bring a real treat for Milkmen, a fabulous reel on JOBS dairy from the 60s! Then tips from 1951, a brilliant reel on the ABC Film Review magazine.
The Hazard of the Die. 1988. Stars Robert Powell, Christopher Scoular & Gavin Richards. Hannay saves the life of a desperate woman, not knowing this act of bravery will bring him to his enemy.
Think Thin. 1981. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Robert thinks that he, Angie and Ida need to lose weight, so he plans a diet for them all to follow. Ida is not happy!
A Corpse in Room 13. 1955. Stars Margaret Anderson & Phil Brown. A wealthy gentleman checks into a hotel and ends up dead. In financial desperation, the hotelier makes a series of bad decisions.
The Case of the Monks Hood Murder. 1964. Stars John Barrie, William Gaunt & Charles Morgan. Mrs. Vincent makes a complaint that Dr. Sneddon murdered her husband, he said he died of consumption.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Dailey & Wayne, Roger Kitter & Johnny Hackett play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1978. Comedy. Director: Stanley Long. Stars Chris Neil, Christopher Biggins, Lindy Benson. Plumber's mate Sid meets ladies at work. Susie is unaware of his dalliances as he gets into debt trouble.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
